mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the federal funds rate raise the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, defined by the unemployment rate rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, signals an impending economic contraction that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict recessions with > 70% accuracy over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of housing starts and industrial production captures the real-economy demand-supply balance; when housing starts exceed 1.2 million units while industrial production remains above 100, robust construction activity coincides with stable manufacturing output, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative predictive value below 40% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns do not differ significantly from the market average when housing and manufacturing diverge.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of disinflation, a stable federal funds rate reduces real borrowing costs without triggering stagflation fears, allowing corporate margins to expand and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL declines while FEDFUNDS remains flat, or the signal predicts losses in > 50% of disinflationary episodes over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following each data release.
failure_condition: The sign of quarterly GDP growth fails to predict the direction of 5-day forward returns with accuracy exceeding 55% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in industrial production below the 98.0 index level signals severe supply-side bottlenecks or demand destruction in the manufacturing sector, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO drops below 98.0, or the threshold breach fails to precede market drawdowns in > 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 200,000 per month combined with a stable unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending without triggering inflationary wage spirals, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when payrolls surge but unemployment ticks up, or the interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment, rising residential construction activity coupled with expanding consumer credit balances fuels household wealth effects and durable goods purchases, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when RSAFS rises but HOUST stagnates, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during periods where credit growth decouples from housing activity.
